title: Incomplete namespace types crashed cursor repaint
summary: Leaving a CUDA source row containing a temporarily incomplete namespace-qualified template argument caused the presentation frontend to index a nil display name during CursorMoved.
details: The reported entry.cu failure appeared when j moved the cursor away from an edited row and the pointer module repainted the newly hidden source.
The failure was independent of the CUDA toolkit: it occurred entirely inside the Lua presentation pipeline after Tree-sitter supplied a partial type fragment.
The recursive type renderer shortened std:: and dans:: names with a pattern that required at least one character after the namespace separator.
For an incomplete fragment such as std:: inside std::vector<std::>, that pattern returned nil and the later alias pass called gsub on the nil value.
The correction preserves namespace-only fragments verbatim until an identifier is present, keeping the pure type parser safe throughout live editing.
A headless interaction regression edits a valid CUDA signature into the partial form while its row is revealed, presses j, delivers CursorMoved, and verifies both source bytes and source-row count.

provenance.reference: conversation 2026-07-23 entry.cu CursorMoved report and Screenshot_20260723_011545.png
provenance.note: Owner reported the parse.lua:554 failure after pressing j in cuda/sampler/entry.cu on a machine without CUDA installed.
A minimized std::vector<std::> live-edit transition reproduced the exact stack independently of CUDA installation.

evidence.claim: The pure grammar preserves a namespace-only fragment, and a real j/CursorMoved transition over partial CUDA syntax completes without an error while preserving bytes and rows.

aliases: pressing j in CUDA file crashes
questions: Why did pressing j after editing a CUDA template type raise parse.lua:554?
Does the CUDA frontend require an installed CUDA toolkit to navigate .cu files?
How does the type renderer handle an incomplete std:: fragment?

last_verified.method: Exact screenshot stack traced to the recursive type renderer, reproduced with a partial std:: template argument, and covered by pure-parser plus deterministic cursor interaction assertions.
